title: AI Product Management
author: Rohit Swerashi
_note: Single source of truth for chapter/lesson TITLES and order. Lesson CONTENT lives in chapters/chNN.js. A lesson shows 'live' only when its content exists in a chapter file, else 'soon'. Keep ids identical between this file and chapter keys. 'through_line' is the one argument every lesson in a chapter must reinforce.

chapters.title: Start Here
chapters.through_line: This is a practical field guide to doing the AI PM job, not theory to memorize — read it, do the reps, and ship the proof.

lessons.t: How to Use This Guide

chapters.title: AI System Design for PMs
chapters.through_line: The model is rarely the bottleneck — retrieval, lever-choice, and unit economics are.

lessons.t: What 'System Design' Means for an AI PM
RAG, End to End — Why Retrieval Is the Bottleneck
Prompt vs RAG vs Fine-tune: The Decision Tree
Scaling, Latency, Cost & Failure Modes

chapters.title: Agentic Architecture for PMs
chapters.through_line: Agency is a liability you add on purpose, not a feature you switch on.

lessons.t: What an Agent Actually Is (Loop, Tools, Memory)
Single-agent vs Multi-agent: When Each Wins
Tool Use, MCP & the Orchestration Layer

chapters.title: How It Works Under the Hood (DSA & Data, the smart way)
chapters.through_line: A data structure is a tradeoff that becomes a user-visible behavior — a miss, a lag, a stale or shallow answer.

lessons.t: Search & Sort — Why Vector DBs Use Approximate, Not Exact
Embeddings, Vector DBs & Indexes (PM-level)
Trees, Graphs & Why They Matter for AI Products

chapters.title: Product Research for AI
chapters.through_line: In an AI-saturated market the scarce thing is a real problem, not a model — find the workflow worth automating, not the demo worth tweeting.

lessons.t: Finding the Right Problem in an AI-saturated Market
User Research When the Product Is Probabilistic
Sizing the AI Opportunity — Build, Wait, or Wrap

chapters.title: Product Analytics & AI Metrics
chapters.through_line: DAU lies for AI products; you measure quality, cost-per-task, and trust, or you're flying blind.

lessons.t: Beyond DAU: Evals, Quality, Cost-per-task
Instrumenting an LLM Product

chapters.title: Product Planning & Prioritization
chapters.through_line: You're roadmapping on shifting ground — plan around the model improving under you, not against a frozen spec.

lessons.t: Roadmapping When the Model Keeps Changing
Prioritizing AI Bets (RICE for Probabilistic Features)
Managing Model-Dependency Risk

chapters.title: AI PRDs & Documentation
chapters.through_line: A PRD for a probabilistic feature specifies the quality bar, the evals, and the failure behavior — not a list of deterministic requirements.

lessons.t: Writing a PRD for a Non-deterministic Feature
Speccing Evals & Acceptance Criteria

chapters.title: Cross-functional Communication
chapters.through_line: Credibility with ML engineers comes from asking the right question, not from pretending to know the answer.

lessons.t: Talking to ML Engineers Without Faking It

chapters.title: Prototyping & Vibe Coding
chapters.through_line: In 2026 a PM who can ship a working demo in a day wins arguments that slides never could.

lessons.t: The 2026 Prototyping Toolkit (v0, Lovable, Cursor, Claude)
Build a Working AI Prototype, Start to Finish
Prototype vs Production — Knowing the Line

chapters.title: Prompting as a PM Skill
chapters.through_line: The prompt is the spec — writing one precisely is product definition, not a party trick.

lessons.t: The Prompt Is the Spec
Prompt Patterns & Iteration

chapters.title: AI Product Improvement Loops
chapters.through_line: AI products improve by closing the eval loop from user feedback to iteration, not by waiting for a bigger model.

lessons.t: Eval-driven Iteration
The Data Flywheel

chapters.title: Customer Comms & GTM for AI
chapters.through_line: You sell a probability, not a guarantee — set expectations honestly or churn punishes you later.

lessons.t: Selling Probabilistic Outcomes Honestly

chapters.title: AI PM Case Interviews
chapters.through_line: The 2026 AI PM case rewards system-mapping and tradeoff-naming over feature brainstorming.

lessons.t: The 2026 AI PM Case Framework
Worked Case: Design an AI Agent / Product
Worked Case: Fix a Broken RAG Product + a Metrics Case

chapters.title: Portfolio & Positioning for 2026 Roles
chapters.through_line: One shipped artifact beats ten bullet points — reading sharpens you, but a portfolio piece gets you hired.

lessons.t: Positioning Yourself for AI PM Roles in 2026
Building the One Artifact That Gets You Hired
The 30-Day Plan: From Reading to Offer

chapters.title: The Case Lab — 10 Real Product Teardowns
chapters.through_line: Frameworks are theory until you run them on a real product; this is where you practice applying them on demand.

lessons.t: Cursor — When 'Cheap Per Task' Lied
Perplexity — Retrieval as the Moat
Lovable — Speed-to-Demo as a Wedge
v0 — A Narrow Wedge With Built-in Distribution
Bolt.new — In-Browser Execution as the Bet
Granola — One Workflow, Done Extremely Well
Gamma — Selling Time-to-Output
ElevenLabs — Quality Bar as the Product
Cluely — Positioning, Virality & the Ethics Line
Replit Agent — Autonomy and the Leash
